description: Training Data & Math Resource Corpus for SWEObeyMe
purpose: Provide comprehensive resources for understanding how LLMs are trained, what training data does to the model, how information is encoded in weights, how gradients shape representational geometry, and what must be preserved for quality to remain intact during FDQ transforms.

categories.name: training_data_theory
categories.description: Training data theory covering tokenization, distributional semantics, scaling laws, and how training data shapes internal representations

documents.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
documents.author: Jared Kaplan, Sam McCandlish, Tom Henighan, Tom B. Brown, Benjamin Chess, Rewon Child, Scott Gray, Alec Radford, Jeffrey Wu, Dario Amodei
documents.description: Foundational paper on scaling laws for neural language models, investigating how performance depends on model size, compute budget, and dataset size
documents.relevance: Understanding how model size and data scale affects training efficiency and final performance

documents.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
documents.author: Jordan Hoffmann, Sebastian Borgeaud, Arthur Mensch, et al.
documents.description: Chinchilla paper establishing optimal compute allocation between model size and training tokens, showing current models are undertrained
documents.relevance: Understanding optimal balance between model size and training data for efficient training

documents.title: SentencePiece: A simple and language independent subword tokenizer and detokenizer
documents.author: Taku Kudo, John Richardson
documents.description: SentencePiece subword tokenizer implementation that treats tokenization as a data preprocessing task, enabling language-independent tokenization
documents.relevance: Understanding how tokenization affects model training and representation

documents.title: Fast WordPiece Tokenization
documents.author: Biao Zhang, Rico Sennrich
documents.description: Efficient algorithms for WordPiece tokenization used in BERT, from single-word tokenization to general text tokenization
documents.relevance: Understanding WordPiece tokenization algorithm used in many LLMs

categories.name: training_math
categories.description: Core training mathematics including gradient descent, backpropagation, loss functions, weight initialization, and numerical stability

documents.title: The Backpropagation algorithm for a math student
documents.author: Various
documents.description: Mathematical treatment of backpropagation algorithm explaining how gradients are computed efficiently through composite structure of neural networks
documents.relevance: Understanding mathematical foundation of how gradients encode information during training

documents.title: An overview of gradient descent optimization algorithms
documents.author: Sebastian Ruder
documents.description: Comprehensive overview of gradient descent variants including SGD, momentum, Adam, RMSProp, and their properties
documents.relevance: Understanding different optimization algorithms and their impact on training dynamics

documents.title: Cross-Entropy Loss Functions: Theoretical Analysis and Applications
documents.author: Zhang, Sabuncu
documents.description: Theoretical analysis of cross-entropy loss functions, their properties, and applications in training deep neural networks
documents.relevance: Understanding loss function behavior and how it affects gradient flow

documents.title: Weight Initialization Techniques for Deep Learning Algorithms
documents.author: Various
documents.description: Overview of weight initialization methods including Xavier/Glorot and Kaiming/He initialization, their theory and impact on training
documents.relevance: Understanding how initialization affects gradient flow and training stability

categories.name: optimization_theory
categories.description: Optimization theory covering convex vs non-convex optimization, saddle points, learning rate schedules, and modern optimizers

documents.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
documents.author: Diederik P. Kingma, Jimmy Ba
documents.description: Adam optimizer paper introducing adaptive moment estimation for stochastic optimization with regret bounds
documents.relevance: Understanding Adam optimizer behavior and its impact on training dynamics

categories.name: representation_learning
categories.description: Representation learning covering embedding space geometry, attention head specialization, superposition theory, and feature interference

documents.title: Toy Models of Superposition
documents.author: Chris Olah, Neel Nanda, et al. (Anthropic)
documents.description: Anthropic paper providing toy models for understanding superposition - how neural networks pack many unrelated concepts into single neurons (polysemanticity)
documents.relevance: Understanding how information is encoded in neural networks and what must be preserved during quantization

documents.title: Interpreting Transformers Through Attention Head Specialization
documents.author: Various
documents.description: Paper on interpreting attention head specialization in transformers, understanding how different heads learn different functions
documents.relevance: Understanding attention head behavior and which patterns must be preserved during FDQ transforms

categories.name: training_pipelines
categories.description: Training pipeline documentation covering sequence packing, masking strategies, batching, and data preprocessing

documents.title: Efficient Sequence Packing without Cross-contamination: Accelerating Large Language Models without Impacting Performance
documents.author: Mario Michael Krell, Matej Kosec, Sergio P. Perez, Andrew Fitzgibbon
documents.description: Paper on efficient sequence packing for training large language models, avoiding cross-contamination while maintaining mathematical equivalence
documents.relevance: Understanding how training pipelines structure data and what this means for model internal representations

documents.title: Learning Better Masking for Better Language Model Pre-training
documents.author: Dongjie Yang, et al.
documents.description: Paper on learning better masking strategies for masked language modeling pre-training to improve model quality
documents.relevance: Understanding how masking strategies during training affect what the model learns

categories.name: training_runs
categories.description: Full training run documentation from real-world LLM training including learning rate curves, loss curves, gradient norms, and numeric behavior

documents.title: BLOOM: A 176B-Parameter Open-Access Multilingual Language Model
documents.author: BigScience Workshop: Teven Le Scao, et al. (392 authors)
documents.description: BigScience BLOOM training paper documenting training of 176B parameter multilingual model on ROOTS corpus, including training methodology and results
documents.relevance: Understanding real-world training process and numeric behavior at large scale

documents.title: The Llama 3 Herd of Models
documents.author: Llama Team, AI @ Meta
documents.description: Meta's Llama 3 training documentation including data curation, training methodology, quality ranking, and multilingual token balancing
documents.relevance: Understanding modern LLM training methodology and data quality practices

documents.title: The Falcon Series of Open Language Models
documents.author: Ebtesam Almazrouei, et al.
documents.description: Technology Innovation Institute's Falcon series training paper covering 7B, 40B, and 180B parameter models trained on diverse high-quality corpora
documents.relevance: Understanding training methodology and data quality requirements for competitive open models

categories.name: data_quality_distribution
categories.description: Data quality and distribution theory covering Zipf's law, token frequency distributions, long-tail effects, and rare token behavior

documents.title: Zipf's Law and Vocabulary
documents.author: Various
documents.description: Review of Zipf's law and its implications for vocabulary and word frequency distributions in natural language
documents.relevance: Understanding how token frequency distributions affect model training and what must be preserved for rare tokens

integrationStatus.training_data_theory: Complete - Core scaling laws and tokenization papers downloaded
integrationStatus.training_math: Complete - Backpropagation, gradient descent, loss functions, and initialization papers downloaded
integrationStatus.optimization_theory: Partial - Adam optimizer downloaded, need more optimizer and learning rate schedule papers
integrationStatus.representation_learning: Complete - Superposition and attention head interpretability papers downloaded
integrationStatus.training_pipelines: Complete - Sequence packing and masking strategy papers downloaded
integrationStatus.training_runs: Complete - BLOOM, Llama 3, and Falcon training documentation downloaded
integrationStatus.data_quality_distribution: Partial - Zipf's law review downloaded, need more token frequency and data quality papers

instructions.addingDocuments: Place resources in appropriate subdirectories. Update this index.json with document metadata including title, author, description, license, topics, and relevance.
instructions.downloadingSources: Download from arXiv, official vendor documentation, technical blogs, and open-source repositories. Prioritize papers with clear mathematical foundations and practical implementation details.
instructions.namingConvention: Use clear, descriptive filenames with underscores: paper_name.pdf, guide_name.html, specification_name.pdf
instructions.licenseCompliance: Include license information for all resources. Prioritize open-access papers, official vendor documentation, and properly licensed content.
instructions.trainingFocus: Resources should enable SWEObeyMe to: (1) understand how training shapes model geometry, (2) identify which tensors are most sensitive to distortion, (3) predict how FDQ affects embeddings, attention, MLPs, and residual streams, (4) preserve all learned structure while shrinking size, (5) reason about deeper FDQ transforms safely, (6) avoid precision collapse by understanding training dynamics.
